reasoning: AICO (Autoridades Indígenas de Colombia) is a small political movement representing indigenous communities in Colombia. It typically campaigns specifically for the two seats in the Senate that are constitutionally reserved for indigenous constituencies. In the 2022 elections, major political forces like the Historic Pact, Conservative Party, and Liberal Party won between 13 and 20 seats each, while AICO secured just one seat. The prospect of AICO expanding its vote share by orders of magnitude to outcompete all major national parties and coalitions and win the most seats overall in the 2026 Senate election is historically unprecedented and politically implausible. Its status as a specialized minority party effectively prevents it from commanding the national majorities required to win a 108-seat chamber. Therefore, the probability is set at the lowest allowable threshold of 2%.
